title: What is an ETF?
content: An ETF, or exchange-traded fund, is a basket of securities such as stocks or bonds that trades on an exchange like a stock. ETFs can provide diversification, lower expense ratios, and easy intraday trading.

title: What is compound interest?
content: Compound interest is interest earned on both the initial principal and the accumulated interest from prior periods. Over long time horizons, compounding can significantly increase savings or investment growth.

title: What is diversification?
content: Diversification means spreading investments across different assets, sectors, or geographies to reduce the impact of poor performance from a single investment.

title: What is inflation?
content: Inflation is the rate at which the prices of goods and services rise over time, which reduces purchasing power. Investments often aim to outpace inflation over the long term.

title: What is dollar-cost averaging?
content: Dollar-cost averaging is an investment strategy where a fixed amount is invested at regular intervals regardless of market price. This can reduce the effect of short-term market volatility.
